sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content: Systemic therapy is the primary treatment for metastatic germ cell tumors and is based on cisplatin combination chemotherapy. The IGCCCG risk classification system guides treatment intensity. For stage I nonseminoma, adjuvant chemotherapy with one cycle of BEP reduces relapse risk. Testicular GCTs are highly chemosensitive with cure rates of approximately 90% for good-risk disease [MS-23].

cards.name: Stage I Nonseminoma Adjuvant
cards.detail: 5-year relapse rate 3.2% with LVI, 1.6% without LVI. Survival exceeds 98%. One cycle preferred due to lower toxicity compared to 2 cycles [MS-18]. Alternatives: Surveillance, Nerve-sparing RPLND. Biomarker requirements: Normal post-orchiectomy AFP and beta-hCG.

cards.name: Stage IS Nonseminoma
cards.detail: Elevated tumor markers indicate occult metastatic disease requiring systemic therapy [NSEM-2]. No alternatives listed. Biomarker requirements: Persistently elevated or rising AFP and/or beta-hCG post-orchiectomy.

cards.name: Stage IIA/IIB Nonseminoma (normal markers)
cards.detail: Standard first-line chemotherapy for good-risk metastatic disease [NSEM-3]. Alternatives: Nerve-sparing RPLND. Biomarker requirements: Normal post-orchiectomy markers.

cards.name: Good-Risk Metastatic (Stages IS, IIA-IIC, IIIA)
cards.detail: Both are category 1 preferred regimens with approximately 90% cure rate. No statistically significant difference in OS (96% vs 92%; P=.096) [MS-23]. Alternatives: EP x4 if bleomycin contraindication. Biomarker requirements: AFP <1,000 ng/mL, hCG <5,000 IU/L, LDH <1.5x ULN for nonseminoma.

cards.name: Intermediate-Risk Metastatic (Stage IIIB)
cards.drugs: Etoposide, Cisplatin, Bleomycin (or Etoposide, Ifosfamide, Cisplatin)
cards.detail: Approximately 70% cure rate [MS-23]. Alternatives: VIP x4 (category 1) for bleomycin contraindication, BEP x3 or EP x4 if intermediate risk based solely on LDH 1.5-3x ULN. Biomarker requirements: AFP 1,000-10,000 ng/mL or hCG 5,000-50,000 IU/L or LDH 1.5-10x ULN.

cards.name: Poor-Risk Metastatic (Stage IIIC)
cards.drugs: Etoposide, Cisplatin, Bleomycin (or Etoposide, Ifosfamide, Cisplatin)
cards.detail: Less than 50% achieve durable complete response. Referral to high-volume center recommended [MS-23]. Alternatives: VIP x4 (category 1) for bleomycin contraindication. Biomarker requirements: AFP >10,000 ng/mL or hCG >50,000 IU/L or LDH >10x ULN or nonpulmonary visceral metastases or mediastinal primary.

cards.name: Good-Risk Metastatic Seminoma
cards.detail: Standard treatment for good-risk seminoma. Consider intensification for LDH >2.5x ULN [MS-15]. Alternatives: EP x4 if bleomycin contraindication. Biomarker requirements: No nonpulmonary visceral metastases, normal AFP.

cards.name: Intermediate-Risk Metastatic Seminoma
cards.drugs: Etoposide, Cisplatin, Bleomycin (or Etoposide, Ifosfamide, Cisplatin)
cards.detail: Stage IIIC with non-pulmonary visceral metastases [MS-15]. Alternatives: VIP x4 for bleomycin contraindication. Biomarker requirements: Non-pulmonary visceral metastases (bone, liver, brain).

cards.name: Second-Line Chemotherapy (Relapsed GCTs)
cards.drugs: Paclitaxel, Ifosfamide, Cisplatin; Vinblastine, Ifosfamide, Cisplatin; or High-dose Carboplatin, Etoposide
cards.detail: Preferred conventional-dose regimens. High-dose chemotherapy based on retrospective data showing superiority over conventional-dose [MS-25]. Alternatives: Clinical trial, High-dose carboplatin/etoposide with stem cell support, Paclitaxel/ifosfamide followed by high-dose carboplatin/etoposide. Biomarker requirements: None specific; treatment based on relapse status.

cards.name: Third-Line Chemotherapy
cards.drugs: High-dose Carboplatin, Etoposide; Gemcitabine, Oxaliplatin, Paclitaxel; Gemcitabine, Oxaliplatin; Gemcitabine, Paclitaxel; Oral Etoposide; Various targeted agents
cards.detail: Clinical trial preferred for patients who received prior high-dose chemotherapy [SEM-8, NSEM-10]. Alternatives: Conventional-dose third-line chemotherapy, Gemcitabine/oxaliplatin/paclitaxel, Gemcitabine/oxaliplatin, Gemcitabine/paclitaxel, Oral etoposide, Pan-cancer tumor-agnostic treatments for actionable mutations. Biomarker requirements: Molecular testing including NGS and IHC for biomarker-driven therapy.

timeline.title: 1st Line
timeline.body: BEP x1 (adjuvant), BEP x3 or EP x4 (good-risk), BEP x4 or VIP x4 (intermediate/poor-risk), BEP x3 or EP x4 (seminoma good-risk), BEP x4 or VIP x4 (seminoma intermediate-risk)

timeline.title: 2nd Line
timeline.body: TIP x4, VeIP x4, High-dose Carboplatin/Etoposide with stem cell support

timeline.title: 3rd Line
timeline.body: High-dose chemotherapy (if not previously received), Clinical trial, Gemcitabine/Oxaliplatin/Paclitaxel, Gemcitabine/Oxaliplatin, Gemcitabine/Paclitaxel, Oral Etoposide, Tumor-agnostic targeted therapy
